scope: Read-only root-cause trace of the failed drift-repair run that later received queue-only workflow events.

sourceTrace.finding: The auto-repair idempotency key is deterministic from the normalized pending-path fingerprint.
A failed drift-repair is not active; after the 15-minute cooldown, the same path set can enqueue the same deterministic idempotency key again.
ensureExecutionRecord collapses every task sharing an idempotency key onto the existing execution record regardless of terminal status.
onEnqueue emits ingress/accepted and queue/queued for the new task id while attaching both events to the reused logical run id.
Startup reconciliation inspects only pending and running execution rows; later enqueue task ids with no execution row are invisible to reconciliation.
The queue is in-memory FIFO with concurrency two; enqueue listeners run before the task is added to p-queue.

rootCause.proven: Run identity and queue-attempt identity are conflated. Re-enqueueing a terminal idempotency key reuses the old failed execution row but emits fresh accepted/queued events for new task ids. Those task ids have no execution or history row, so persisted telemetry can claim queued work that has no independently reconcilable execution state.
contributingFactors: Deterministic doc-repair keys are reused after the cooldown when the pending path fingerprint is unchanged.
Failed executions are excluded from the active-repair guard.
Queue admission and queue telemetry are not an atomic persisted operation.
FIFO queue pressure can starve repair tasks behind bulk doc-change fan-out.
rootCause.highConfidenceInference: The two later attempts remained behind queue pressure and were lost at a later process restart before processing. This is supported by the queue-only event sequence, absent execution/history rows, the large event-window backlog, later startup reconciliation, and the in-memory FIFO design. The exact removal moment cannot be proven because historical in-memory queue snapshots were not persisted.

minimumCodeSlice: Add a central idempotency admission result before accepted/queued telemetry is emitted.
Suppress duplicate keys for pending, running, success, and failed executions; record duplicate-suppressed telemetry instead of accepted/queued.
Permit same-key requeue only for an explicit retrying execution with a matching persisted retry-recovery record.
Persist a distinct queue-attempt identity for every admitted retry so restart reconciliation can close it deterministically.
Emit accepted/queued only after queue admission succeeds.
Re-check active repair and cooldown state after acquiring the doc-repair lock to close the known concurrent threshold race.
requiredTests: failed terminal key is suppressed without changing the original failure
pending and running duplicate keys are suppressed
explicit retrying plus persisted recovery is admitted and linked
restart reconciliation cannot leave queue-only task ids without execution or suppression evidence
concurrent doc-change threshold handlers create only one repair admission

limitations: Session-transcript semantic search timed out; durable memory and local evidence were used instead.
coding-agent-skills is static audit-only and could not inspect protected runtime state; narrow read-only SQLite and source inspection were required.
No historical in-memory queue snapshot exists, so the precise disposal moment is inferred rather than directly observed.
approvalBoundaries: source implementation beyond this decision artifact
test/build execution if it materially stresses the host
nextSafeStep: Implement the bounded admission-and-telemetry test slice in the canonical project without touching retained history; review the diff and tests before any runtime restart or deployment.
